methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $575M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
VulcanForms is a late-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $575M+ to date. The comparable universe in Robotics & Manufacturing includes 1 public company (277810.KQ) and 8 private peers, among them 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tiers to support a reliable median multiple approach; only three or more tight comparables would enable that method.

Consequently, stage-progression analysis provides the primary valuation framework. Using the $575M in total capital raised as a proxy for recent post-money value, a 1.2x step-up multiplier applicable to late-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $690M. This method is grounded in typical late-stage financing dynamics, though it carries inherent uncertainty given the absence of granular peer multiples.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to one applicable method, supports a median valuation of $690M, with a low-end range of $586M (approximately 1.02x raised capital) and a high-end range of $793M (approximately 1.38x raised capital). Key uncertainties include the absence of tight-fitting peer comparables, reliance on raised capital as a valuation proxy rather than disclosed valuations, and the broad range span. Validation would benefit from additional late-stage robotics comparables or disclosed metrics from the peer set.
